package main

import (
	"database/sql"
	"fmt"

	"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Bootstrap mixes gorm.Open and sql.Raw without an adapter boundary.
// The application initializes both GORM and raw sql.DB and passes them directly
// to repositories, creating a tangled persistence layer with no abstraction.

func bootstrapDSN(dsn string) (*gorm.DB, *sql.DB, error) {
	gormDB, err := gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{})
	if err != nil {
		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("gorm open: %w", err)
	}

	rawDB, err := sql.Open("postgres", dsn)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("sql open: %w", err)
	}

	// Both connections handed out directly — no adapter, no boundary.
	return gormDB, rawDB, nil
}

type User struct {
	ID   uint   `gorm:"primaryKey"`
	Name string `gorm:"column:name"`
}

type UserRepository struct {
	gormDB *gorm.DB
	rawDB  *sql.DB
}

func (r *UserRepository) FindByIDGORM(id uint) (User, error) {
	var u User
	err := r.gormDB.First(&u, id).Error
	return u, err
}

func (r *UserRepository) FindByRawSQL(id uint) (User, error) {
	var u User
	row := r.rawDB.QueryRow("SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE id = $1", id)
	err := row.Scan(&u.ID, &u.Name)
	return u, err
}

func main() {
	dsn := "host=localhost user=postgres password=secret dbname=mydb port=5432 sslmode=disable"
	gormDB, rawDB, _ := bootstrapDSN(dsn)

	repo := &UserRepository{gormDB: gormDB, rawDB: rawDB}
	_ = repo
}
